{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "tested/inspected", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 90, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Control wiring is installed in the same raceway as power wiring for VFD/motor controls", "Control and power conductors are not separated, creating electromagnetic interference (EMI) and fault hazards", "Installation does not comply with separation requirements for VFD control and power circuits"], "actions": ["Remove control wiring from the power raceway", "Install control wiring in a separate raceway or use approved barrier/segregation method per NEC and manufacturer instructions", "Inspect and test VFD control signals and motor operation after separation", "Verify proper shielding and grounding of control cables per OEM manual"], "rationale": "Control wiring in the same raceway as power wiring violates NEC separation practices for motor control circuits and VFD manufacturer installation requirements. This can cause EMI, erratic VFD operation, premature failure, and safety hazards, so the installation is defective and non-compliant.", "references": ["NEC 430", "mfr manuals"]}
